'''Ben Crooks''' (born 15 June 1993) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].

Crooks has previously played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from Hull at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He was contracted to [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[NRL]], returning to the UK to play for [[Castleford Tigers]], and then on loan from Castleford at  [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Crooks was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire]], England. He is the [[son]] of Hull F.C. player, [[Lee Crooks (rugby league)|Lee Crooks]] and the [[nephew]] of another Hull F.C. Legend, [[Steve Norton]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=June 6, 2023 |title=Ben Crooks told Boots 'n' All about comparisons with dad Lee |url=https://www.skysports.com/watch/news/8794244/ben-crooks-told-boots-n-all-about-comparisons-with-dad-lee |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20240509122853/https://www.skysports.com/watch/news/8794244/ben-crooks-told-boots-n-all-about-comparisons-with-dad-lee |archive-date=May 9, 2024 |access-date=May 5, 2024 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Hull F.C. (2012–14)===
Crooks is a product of the [[Hull F.C.]] Academy System.  Crooks made his Hull F.C. and [[Super League]] début on 22 April 2012, in a 56–12 loss to [[Wigan Warriors]].

He enjoyed a breakthrough season in 2013, creating a deadly try-scoring combination with {{rlp|wg}}er [[Tom Lineham]]. Which led to his selection in the [[Super League Dream Team]]. At the end of the 2013 season, he was named [[Super League]]'s [[Man of Steel Awards|'Young Player of the Year]].'

He also represented [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[2013 Challenge Cup]] Final.  Crooks struggled to recapture his form in 2014, making only 12 first-team appearances for [[Hull F.C.]] and spending time on loan at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]].{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}
[[File:Ben Crooks.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Crooks in action for [[Hull F.C.]] in 2014]]

===Doncaster R.L.F.C. (2014)===
He had a short loan-spell at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in 2014, making three appearances and scoring five tries in total.

===Parramatta Eels (2015)===
Crooks signed a two-year deal with [[National Rugby League]] side [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]], in September 2014. But he left after only a year, after his deal with [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] turned out to be an unsuccessful one.

As a result, Crooks mainly played for the reserve-grade [[New South Wales Cup]] team the [[Wentworthville Magpies]].<ref name="Crooks Signs New Deal">{{cite news|url=http://www.thisishullandeastriding.co.uk/Hull-FC-s-Ben-Crooks-agrees-new-year-extension/story-16687980-detail/story.html#axzz2Ll674qBe|title=Crooks Signs New Deal|newspaper=Hull Daily Mail|date=2012-08-10|accessdate=2008-10-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120814081158/http://www.thisishullandeastriding.co.uk/Hull-FC-s-Ben-Crooks-agrees-new-year-extension/story-16687980-detail/story.html#axzz2Ll674qBe|archive-date=14 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Subsequently, following his departure from [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]], Crooks signed a deal to play for [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[Super League]], ahead of the 2016 [[rugby league]] season.

===Castleford Tigers (2016–17)===
Crooks returned to the [[Super League]] in 2016 and signed a three-year contract with [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. Although he didn't manage to play in the [[NRL]] he shared his belief saying that, "I believe I have achieved my goals in my career development and I will return to the [[Super League]] a better player for the experience."<ref>{{cite news  | title = Castleford Tigers sign former Hull FC centre | publisher = BBC Sport | date = 2015-06-27 | url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/33297854 | accessdate = 2015-08-21}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions (2017–18)===
Crooks joined [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] on loan from [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] for the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37900341|title=Ben Crooks: Leigh Centurions sign Castleford Tigers centre on loan|publisher=BBC Sport|date=7 November 2016|accessdate=7 December 2017}}</ref>

The move was then made on a permanent basis with a two-year deal signed in October 2017.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41499077|title=Ben Crooks: Leigh Centurions sign centre from Castleford following loan spell|publisher=BBc Sport|date=4 October 2017|accessdate=7 December 2017}}</ref>
[[File:Ben Crooks Leigh.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Crooks in action for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in 2018]]

===Hull Kingston Rovers (2018–22)===
It was revealed on 26 July 2018, that Crooks would be returning to the [[Kingston upon Hull|city of Hull]] to play for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] for the remainder of the 2018 [[rugby league]] season, on a loan basis from Leigh.

The loan-deal which involved Crooks moving to the [[Kingston upon Hull|east Hull]] outfit, also saw his fellow Centurions' teammate [[Craig Hall (rugby league, born 1988)|Craig Hall]] come over with him in the same move.

On 27 July 2018, just a day after joining Hull Kingston Rovers. Crooks made his début for the club on a rain-swept evening against Hull F.C. at the [[KCOM Stadium]], in the final round of the regular [[Super League XXIII|Super League]] season.

Crooks marked his return to the city of Hull, starting the game at the {{rlp|ce}} position in the [[Rugby league derbies in the United Kingdom|'Hull Derby]].' In which Hull KR claimed a 16–20 victory over their cross-city rivals.

It was revealed on 24 October 2018, that Crooks had agreed to sign a new two-year contract, to make his loan move from Leigh to Hull Kingston Rovers on a permanent basis starting in 2019.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/ben-crooks-signs-with-the-club-for-two-years/|title=Ben Crooks signs with the club for two years…|publisher=Hull Kingston Rovers|date=24 October 2018|accessdate=25 October 2018}}</ref>

In round 3 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Crooks scored the fastest try in Super League history after scoring in the opening seven seconds of Hull Kingston Rovers 25–24 victory over Huddersfield.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56734758|title=Hull Kingston Rovers 25-24 Huddersfield Giants - Hull KR score seven-second try|work=BBC Sport |date=14 April 2021 }}</ref>

In round 6 of the 2021 Super League season, he scored two tries in a 26–22 victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3543315|title=Super League scores|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Crooks made a total of 17 appearances for Hull KR in the 2021 Super League season as the club reached the semi-final against the [[Catalans Dragons]] but were defeated 28–10.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final|newspaper=The Guardian|date=30 September 2021 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58663060.amp/|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 0-19 Hull KR - Robins set up a semi-final at Catalans|work=BBC Sport|date=23 September 2021 }}</ref>
On 29 August 2022, it was announced that Crooks would be one of eleven players who were to depart Hull Kingston Rovers at the end of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/hull-kr-confirm-11-departures-including-former-nrl-trio-and-star-winger-58198/|title=Hull KR confirm 11 departures including former NRL trio and star winger|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com|date=28 August 2022 }}</ref>

===Keighley Cougars (2023–)===
Crooks signed a two-year deal with the [[Keighley Cougars]] on 30 September 2022.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Ibbetson |first1=Stephen |title=Keighley Cougars confirm signing of Hull KR star |url=https://www.totalrl.com/keighley-cougars-confirm-signing-of-hull-kr-star/ |access-date=30 September 2022 |work=TotalRL.com |date=30 September 2022}}</ref>

===Halifax===
On 16 June 2023, Crooks signed a contract to join RFL Championship side Halifax.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/halifax-panthers-sign-ben-crooks-nick-rawsthorne-departs/|title=Halifax Panthers sign former Super League back as ex-Toronto Wolfpack ace departs the club|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
